rules.statement: INSERT operations against core.blackboard_entries MUST originate from the Worker base class at src/shared/workers/base.py. Services, atomic actions, and other non-Worker code MUST route blackboard posting through self.post_finding(), self.post_report(), or self.post_heartbeat(). UPDATE operations on already-attributed rows are unaffected.
rules.rationale: The worker_uuid NOT NULL constraint on core.blackboard_entries requires every row to be attributable to a registered Worker. Raw-SQL INSERTs from non-Worker sites bypass the base class's _post_entry method, which fills attribution fields from Worker identity. ADR-011 codifies this principle; this rule enforces it at audit time.

rules.statement: Every UPDATE on core.blackboard_entries that transitions a row to status='awaiting_reaudit' MUST co-occur with the predicate `resolution_mechanism = 'reaudit'` in the same WHERE clause. A SQL site that flips a row to awaiting_reaudit without the resolution_mechanism guard is a constitutional violation: it would park a self_resolve or human finding into ADR-045's sensor re-audit queue, where no audit sensor can adjudicate it because none owns the subject prefix's truth claim.
rules.rationale: ADR-091 D2 Revision B (accepted 2026-06-05) names the load-bearing invariant: a finding may be transitioned to awaiting_reaudit if and only if its resolution_mechanism = 'reaudit'. The runtime guard ships in the same commit at src/body/services/blackboard_service/blackboard_proposal_service.py (revive_findings_for_failed_proposal). This rule polices the SQL surface so any future site that adds another awaiting_reaudit write inherits the guard or fails audit. The check is an AST-over-SQL-strings sweep: locate every UPDATE … SET status = 'awaiting_reaudit' and confirm `resolution_mechanism = 'reaudit'` appears in the WHERE clause of the same statement. Engine implementation is deferred to a follow-up issue per ADR-091 D2 commit 1c scoping; this rule ships as advisory in the interim so the constitutional content is on the books from commit 1c forward. Target enforcement: blocking once the engine lands and one audit cycle confirms zero violations (expected — the only awaiting_reaudit site already carries the guard from this commit).

rules.statement: Every UPDATE on core.blackboard_entries whose SET clause transitions a row to status='indeterminate' (a literal `status = 'indeterminate'` assignment, or a bound `status = :status` parameter that may be 'indeterminate') MUST co-assign `resolution_mechanism = 'human'` in the same SET clause — as a literal, or via `CASE WHEN :status = 'indeterminate' THEN 'human' ELSE resolution_mechanism END`. A finding marked indeterminate is closed by a human governor, never by ADR-045's reaudit sensor; leaving resolution_mechanism at its birth value 'reaudit' parks it in the reaudit queue where no sensor can clear it, and the audit re-detects and re-emits it every cycle.
rules.rationale: ADR-091 D2 Amendment (accepted 2026-06-12, #628) names the field-ownership invariant: a transition that changes which authority class may close a finding owns resolution_mechanism, not just the posting authority at birth. This rule is the symmetric twin of reaudit_requires_reaudit_mechanism — where that guards entry into the reaudit queue, this guards the delegation/indeterminate transition from leaving the reaudit value stale. The runtime fix ships in the same change-set at four UPDATE sites: BlackboardService.mark_indeterminate, BlackboardService.update_entry_status, CallSiteRewriter._mark_findings, and PromptArtifactWriter. The check is an AST-over-SQL-strings sweep scoped to the SET body (statements that merely filter on status='indeterminate' in WHERE are not flagged). Detection differs from the reaudit guard's literal-only match because indeterminate is written both literally and via :status parameter. Shipped as reporting in commit 54eb9965; promoted to blocking 2026-06-12 after #628 fix-D ran (acceptance gate = 0) and one full audit cycle (14× audit_violation_sensor.run.complete) confirmed zero violations — the ramp-arc gate per ADR-079 D10 Stage 2.
